title: runtime/ → session/ — relation/mutate/db/db_battery cut destiny under decisions.md
privilege: Architect cut contract only. decisions.md is sole Spec authority — this file yields on conflict. Current condemned paths appear only as Delete evidence; every seat and rewire destination is a target-tree path. Claim-tag inflation or jointly impossible meters void this plan. Pre-law destinies from deprecated-migrated.md / deprecated-absorbed.md re-derived under the law; corrections recorded as rulings.

scope.this_cut: Condemned: crates/kyzo-core/src/runtime/{relation,mutate,db,db_battery}.rs — the runtime/ directory dies. Living targets: session/{catalog,access,admit,ops,db,jobs,verify,observe}.rs and exec/plan/compile.rs (IndexPositionUse relocation).
scope.not_this_json: query/ (01), storage/ (03), engines/ (04), data+fixed_rule (05), hosts (06), storage-arch seats (07). The §1 Engine/Store/Catalog decomposition of the Db handle is the storage epic's demolition — recorded here as a carried obligation, not executed in this cut.
foundation_already_true: session/{admit,catalog,ops,access,jobs,db(54-line),verify}.rs exist as stubs or partial seats; session/observe.rs landed (212 lines); session/pinned_handle.hex golden bytes present
scope.target_not_current: by_target and allowlist.targets are target-tree paths only. Current condemned paths live solely under deletes / delete_after.

authority: This file — cut destiny; subordinate to decisions.md

claim_tags.law: Mandatory Spec tags from decisions.md only. Every unrepresentable re-tags under exactly one. Inventing a fourth tag or inflating Unexposed to Unconstructible voids this plan.
claim_tags.Unconstructible: No path through the sealed Rust type and crate boundary constructs the illegal state. Proof: private constructor + compile-fail a crate-consumer trybuild can demonstrate.
claim_tags.Refused: Reachable program; typed runtime outcome. Named refuse variants.
claim_tags.Unexposed: Absent from the public API but reachable by a hostile adapter. Name residual risk; real detection or residual accepted.

joint_satisfaction.law: Allowlist ⊇ every TARGET file the meters require to change. Condemned paths are deleted via delete_after — not edited as living seats.
joint_satisfaction.proof_pairing: compile-fail for type-law claims (write-session species, sealed msgpack door); grep-delete for symbols/paths.
joint_satisfaction.no_condemned_rewire: runtime/* paths appear only under deletes/delete_after.

deletes.law: E-action ledger for condemned CURRENT paths.

paths.why: Catalog + handle + serialization boundary → session/catalog.rs; AccessLevel → session/access.rs; IndexPositionUse → exec/plan/compile.rs (its own provisional-home note). File dies.
Mutation pipeline → session/admit.rs (the ALL-writes-one-path clause); index lifecycle → session/ops.rs. File dies.

Entrypoint + SessionTx routing → session/db.rs; sys-op dispatch splits by family — jobs → session/jobs.rs, operator surface → session/ops.rs, catalog ops → session/catalog.rs, Verify → session/verify.rs. File dies.

Absorbed: e2e/contention/determinism arms beside session/db.rs; trigger-cache kill beside session/admit.rs; callback exactly-once arms beside session/observe.rs; F2 pin beside the session router. Named hostile-review sections — independence lives in reviewer authorship and deterministic mutant-killing, not file separation.

symbols.name: amend_key_prefix
symbols.why: Already deleted on port — splicing a relation id into an EncodedKey's bytes launders unproven provenance into the typed key. Grep-guard so it never returns.

symbols.name: lock: bool on read paths
symbols.why: The kernel is full SSI; there is no unlocked read to ask for. Died on port; grep-guard.

symbols.name: process-wide AtomicU64 id counter
symbols.why: Replaced by transactional RMW (leaked ids on abort; could not survive a second process). Grep-guard the pattern at the catalog seat.

not_deletes.why: Gating it on itself would wedge relations shut — the reason is recorded doctrine and travels with the code.

not_deletes.item: D2/N2-style documented quirks in SessionTx routing (retired-id funnel, Segments::OFF in write sessions)
not_deletes.why: Documented pinned behavior with named lineage; preserved, not 'fixed'.
The wire-format conversation-starter — session/pinned_handle.hex is already at its target seat.

refused.law: Typed runtime outcomes. Named variants. Authority honest.

items.name: temp-relation mutation reaching the read path
items.why: Without this refusal the read path would silently drop the mutation — a silent-wrong-answer class.

items.name: trigger cascade exceeding depth 32
items.why: Never silent truncation and never an unbounded loop — typed whole-abort.

items.name: temp relation routed to durable catalog ops
items.why: Refused here rather than routed — is_temp is the routing DATUM, not the router.

items.name: relation id space exhausted
items.why: Typed exhaustion routed through raw_decode's single bounds check — never a wrap.

items.name: insufficient access level
items.why: The Ord derive IS the semantics; refusal names required vs held rung.

items.name: invalid timeout
items.why: The last line of defense before Duration::from_secs_f64 would panic — refuse, never panic, on a reachable input.

items.name: rename with indices attached
items.why: The original stranded index rows under the old name — refusing is the fix's permanent form.

unexposed.law: Residual hostile-adapter risk with real detection or residual accepted.

items.name: hand-serialized catalog row injected below the kernel (shadow-struct corruption)
items.residual_risk: A hostile writer below the session surface plants an out-of-range id or malformed msgpack catalog row.
items.detection: decode refuses typed at first touch (shadow-struct corruption test travels with catalog.rs); storage verify campaign walks catalog rows — the walk continues past the wound and names BadTag

items.name: trigger program cached under a stale key by an in-process adapter
items.residual_risk: Bypassing the per-session parse cache could run a stale trigger program.
items.detection: db_battery's cache-keyed-by-source mutant kill (two on-put triggers each running ITS program) — deterministic campaign, re-homed beside session/admit.rs

carried_obligations.from: decisions.md §1 vs target-arch session/db.rs ('primary Db capability handle')
carried_obligations.obligation: The ambient Db facade (one handle granting storage + schema + evaluation) is condemned by §1. This cut keeps Db as current currency; the storage epic demolishes it into Engine(Store, Catalog) composition. Record at the seat; do not half-rename now.
Triggers parsed once per session is sound (one cur_vld); the Phase C parsed-substances end state (triggers stored parsed) remains open and operator-visible.
No parsed surface for ::temporal index create; Erase has no scripted surface. The direct-SessionTx tests are the documented stopgap; the parsed surface is owed.
The #[allow(clippy::collapsible_if)] toolchain-drift workaround is dated — re-check on the next toolchain bump.

tests.law: Batteries re-home beside their subjects as named hostile-review sections. Never weaken a pinned mutant-killer.

fate.to: session/db.rs tests (rs3_independent_e2e_scenario, 3-writer contention, determinism across backends and repeats, F2 temp-relation pin), session/admit.rs tests (two-put-triggers-fire-distinctly — the cache-keyed-by-source mutant kill), session/observe.rs tests (callbacks exactly-once under contention + under seeded spurious conflicts — the deterministic phantom detector)

session/catalog.rs + session/access.rs module tests (shadow-struct corruption, racing-creates concurrency proof, wire round-trip + PINNED_HANDLE_HEX, access ladder, skip-scan time travel, choose_index edges, ensure_compatible fix pin)

session/admit.rs tests (store-bytes-unchanged pin with meaning anchor, per-row terminal-tick whole-abort, three story-#88 coverage-gap pins, WRITE-COUNT LAW with SimStorage put_call_count oracle) + session/ops.rs tests (backfill-equals-incremental across two universes, base↔posting bijection, posting-rows-match-history with hand-encoded first-key byte claim)

session/db.rs tests (segments law e2e, baseline-forwarding spent=73 pin, retraction-governs pin, magic-sets end-to-end differential obligations 11–12, runaway-recursion + widening default-budget pin, bracketing test)

meters: rg -n 'put_call_count' crates/kyzo-core/src/session → at least 1 (write-count law oracle survived)

authoritative_constructs: SystemKey (closed enum) / RelationHandle / IndexRef / IndexKind (+manifests) / ConstraintRef / KeyspaceKind / catalog ops / sealed msgpack door — session/catalog.rs
IndexPositionUse — exec/plan/compile.rs (per its own provisional-home note)
execute_relation + put/update/remove + ensure/ensure_not + collect_mutations + update_indices + index_write_row + extractors + make_const_rule + trigger cascade — session/admit.rs
attach_and_backfill + five ::create ops + remove_index + IndexCtx + operator sys-ops (Compact, MerkleRoot, index DDL) — session/ops.rs
Db handle + run_script* + execute_single + compile_and_eval + finalize_rows + build_budget + SessionTx routing — session/db.rs

unconstructible.name: mutation against a read session
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail (trybuild): mutation requires SessionTx<T: WriteTx>; a read session does not satisfy the bound

unconstructible.name: a row value routed through the msgpack catalog door
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail: the CatalogRecord supertrait is sealed and the ambiguity-trick test fails the build if DataValue ever implements CatalogRecord

rulings.a: A handle is a decoded catalog row — knowledge, not authority; the store's bytes remain the truth. SSI, no catalog locks, no process-wide atomics: uniqueness is isolation's theorem, not an atomic's side effect. This is §7-adjacent current law and survives into the storage epic where Catalog becomes the Engine-held interpreter.
msgpack struct maps ARE an on-disk format; changing them is a migration conversation under FormatVersion (§81 shape), not a refactor. PINNED_HANDLE_HEX failing is that conversation starting.
ALL writes enter through session/admit.rs — the zone law's one-path clause. update_indices stays the ONE write-side seam cross-module tests drive (pub(crate) at the admit path).

Old and new compose to the IDENTICAL posting key at the IDENTICAL coordinate; dual-fire would silently let the Assert clobber the Retract. No byte-content test can guard it — the guard is the write-count law (SimStorage put_call_count: exactly 2 puts per mutation kind, 0 dels ever). The epistemics travel with the code.

Fresh tx + fresh callback collector per attempt; segment bumps BEFORE commit; retirement evictions AFTER; callbacks after durable. A conflicted attempt leaks no phantom events; a committed one loses nothing.
Kill switch, timeouts, and the deadline half of Budget all set the one CancelFlag; a rule that honors check honors all of them for free.

ban: Do not delete the unconditional SSI probe in put_into_relation — the fact-range read it conflict-tracks is the ONLY thing that makes a same-fact race abort one racer instead of losing an update.
Do not replace the write-count law with a byte-content test — the dual-fire mutant is byte-identical on committed disk.
Do not gate set_access_level on itself.
Do not reorder AccessLevel variants — the Ord derive IS the semantics.
Do not resurrect cleanups machinery, lock: bool, or the process-wide id counter.
Do not route temp relations — refuse them at the catalog door.
Do not re-key the trigger cache by source text alone.
Do not resolve mutation coordinates at any instant but the write's own valid.
Do not turn the retired-id funnel back into three sibling destroy sites.
Do not rename Db to Engine in this cut — that is the storage epic's §1 demolition (carried obligation).
Do not use whole-engine cargo green as this cut's meter.

TARGET session/access.rs ← AccessLevel ladder from condemned runtime/relation.rs

proof_meters: compile-fail (trybuild): mutation on a read session (SessionTx<T: WriteTx> bound unsatisfied)
compile-fail: DataValue implementing CatalogRecord breaks the sealed-door ambiguity trick

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/catalog.rs.kind: TARGET — the store's knowledge of its relations; coherent multi-row moves
ban: catalog locks
process-wide atomics
second serialization door for catalog metadata
amend_key_prefix
carried_notes: SystemKey is a closed enum — no fourth shape can appear by accident; STORAGE_VERSION merged into FormatVersion which refuses strictly-earlier-and-stricter.
Indices BY REFERENCE — the index relation's own catalog row is the single authority.

IndexKind::Temporal design doc: the valid instant promoted ahead of the base key answers 'what changed at/near instant t' with a contiguous scan — scan-shaped, not search-shaped; the leading column is the WRITE'S OWN coordinate. Unit variants grew manifests decode-compatible with every store ever written.
KeyspaceKind: Facts vs AlgorithmState — versioning AlgorithmState would corrupt its invariants.

destroy's del_range runs in-transaction so an aborted transaction rolls the destruction back; rename refuses with indices attached; the two fixes-on-port (ensure_compatible trivially-true self-compare; create_relation dead store + opposite-store name check) keep their pins forever.
choose_index: longest bound prefix, back-join coverage, law-5 edges degrading to no-index.
Scan surface: every method takes the transaction to read; none of them routes — is_temp is the routing DATUM; skip scans strip time slots to LOGICAL rows; zero-clone projected probes.

ban: reordering variants (Ord derive IS the semantics)

crates/kyzo-core/src/exec/plan/compile.r.kind: TARGET living seat — IndexPositionUse relocates here per its provisional-home note
ban: session imports for this type
delete_meter: rg -n 'IndexPositionUse' crates/kyzo-core/src/exec/plan/compile.rs → definition present; zero definitions elsewhere

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/admit.rs.kind: TARGET — the write admission path: mutation enters here only
ban: a second write path outside admit
wall-clock valid defaults (stamp is snapshot-monotone)
physical delete as retraction
dual-fire temporal index writes
carried_notes: THE LOAD-BEARING UNCONDITIONAL SSI PROBE: bitemporal version keys are distinct per transaction stamp, so two writers of the same fact never collide on written keys — the fact-range READ this probe conflict-tracks is the ONLY thing that makes a same-fact race abort one racer instead of losing an update; resolved AT THIS WRITE'S OWN valid, never an unrelated later instant.
VALID-coordinate default: the stamp, not wall-clock — snapshot-monotone, so a retrying writer can never land its update at an instant an already-committed writer has shadowed.
Retraction is revision, not erasure: a Retract row at the coordinate, never a physical delete; the preserved _new-carries-key-columns-only asymmetry.
update_in_relation: must-exist at the write's own valid; carry-forward of omitted non-key columns with a typed short-stored-row error.
:ensure can never carry a @ clause — 'current' always means the newest instant ever recorded.
TEMPORAL SINGLE-FIRE ruling with honest epistemics: content-equivalent to the dual-fire shape on disk, so the guard is the write-count law, not a byte test.
index_write_row: the shared scan-shaped seam writing at the base write's EXACT coordinate; the index's own watermark bump has a demonstrated-stale-read lineage.
IndexCtx resolved once per session and cached — a manifest that no longer parses/builds/decodes is a typed refusal at first touch, never mid-scan corruption.
:replace gates: in-trigger, with-indices, below-Normal; old triggers carried across the replace; touched_relations noted for the segment watermark before commit.

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/ops.rs.kind: TARGET — operator surface: index lifecycle + operator sys-ops
ban: resolution during temporal backfill (resolution is exactly what would collapse the history the backfill must reproduce whole)
carried_notes: Temporal backfill is a raw whole-history walk; plain/manifest backfill walks current rows with the 0xFF group-clearing resume bound and the re-mints-now note; a posting IS a bitemporal fact (KeyspaceKind dispatch).
HNSW ::create documents its standard derivations; LSH builds are pinned-seed byte-identical.
Backfill-equals-incremental is the rebuildability law — two universes, id alignment asserted, raw-byte identity.

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/db.rs.kind: TARGET — the entrypoint: script string to result rows; SessionTx routing core
ban: cooperative-poison threads or sleeping limit enforcement
per-request mutability overrides (mutability read off the parsed program)
second kill flag
carried_notes: DEFAULT_DERIVED_TUPLE_CEILING 50M is not a round guess: bench_api's own ceiling reused, verified against kyzo-bench's recorded real-graph results with headroom arithmetic; the fast-refusing alternative was rejected because it would silently regress already-recorded benchmarks; a fixpoint-less query always crosses it structurally.
MAX_COMMIT_ATTEMPTS 128 with measured liveness reasoning — at 32 it was reachable by three writers under load, which is contention working, not failing.
Ordered write ceremony: fresh tx+collector, run_query, enforce_constraints, segment bumps BEFORE commit, retirement evictions AFTER, callbacks after durable.
Segments::OFF in write sessions is typed dirty-read protection, pinned by the constraint suite.
SessionTx::destroy_relation retired-id FUNNEL: three sibling destroy sites leaked one engine entry per cycle, forever.
The one-machine ruling: the row-at-a-time twin was deleted on measurement; criterion on a loaded 32-core box had it losing or tying everywhere.
Stale-comment mea culpa as standing doctrine: an external audit read a stale claim as ground truth — exactly the failure a comment in this codebase must never cause.
A transaction's writes are one instant of recorded history (system_stamp_routed).

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/verify.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — SysOp::Verify routes here

crates/kyzo-core/src/session/observe.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — callback delivery law + its exactly-once batteries
ban: pre-commit callback delivery
collector reused across retry attempts
carried_notes: The seeded-spurious-conflict phantom detector: replacing a thread race with a deterministic replay is the sharpest form of the exactly-once proof — 40% seeded spurious conflicts force retry replays with no thread races; a collector not rebuilt per attempt, or pre-commit delivery, duplicates events.
Raw-order determinism assertions distinguished from sorted correctness assertions by helper choice (int_rows vs raw_int_rows).

must_add: session/db.rs description gains the §1 obligation note: Db is the pre-storage-epic name of the Engine composition seat

final_meters: write-count law test (put_call_count) present under session
callbacks exactly-once pair present under session/observe.rs tests
PINNED_HANDLE_HEX wire round-trip test present under session

seated_laws.law: all writes enter through the admission path — one write door
seated_laws.meter: rg -n 'fn put_into_relation|fn update_in_relation|fn remove_from_relation' crates/kyzo-core/src/session → all hits inside session/admit.rs

seated_laws.law: no panic on reachable session inputs — typed refusals
seated_laws.meter: rg -n 'panic!|unwrap\(|expect\(' crates/kyzo-core/src/session/{admit,catalog,ops,db,access,jobs,verify}.rs → zero outside cfg(test) and the Law-5-audited constructor-proof sites
exceptions: Law-5-audited sites carried from the fork headers (constructor proofs; store_of-style invariants) — each carries its written justification at the call site

seated_laws.law: temporal single-fire — exactly 2 puts per mutation kind, 0 dels ever
seated_laws.meter: the write-count law test asserts put_call_count == 2 and del_call_count == 0 per mutation kind

qae.q: db_battery's F2 pin ('temp relation mutation is a typed refusal') is scheduled 'beside the session router when temp relations land' — but the router IS session/db.rs now. Does the pin land at db.rs tests immediately, or wait for a temp-relations story?
qae.a: Land it now beside session/db.rs's execute_single tests — the refusal exists today (TempRelationNotReachableError) and the pin guards it today. Waiting for a temp-relations story orphans a live mutant-kill.
qae.e: An unlanded pin on a live refusal is a coverage hole with a name. Close: tests.fate routes the F2 pin to session/db.rs; final meter checks the exactly-once and e2e arms present.

campaigns_proposed.target_seat: decisions.md §41 (read-your-writes across Engines on one Store) — current-law precursor at the session tier
campaigns_proposed.campaign: two Db clones on one universe: A commits, B reads the same cut without reopen; assert visibility with zero reopen; a reopen-required observation falsifies the shared-belief-surface claim at the current seam and informs the §41 DST.
